title: Burgers conservative update changes total by boundary flux mismatch

source.citation: Randall J. LeVeque, Finite Volume Methods for Hyperbolic Problems, Cambridge University Press, 2002, Sec. 11.3, p. 208 and Ch. 4, pp. 64-65.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: The finite total after a Burgers conservative update equals the old finite total minus scale times the boundary Burgers flux mismatch.
assumptions: scale is a real update scale
u is a real-valued grid state on natural-number indices
n is a natural-number range length
informal.expected_conclusion: The total balance for the Burgers conservative update is controlled by boundary flux mismatch.

declarations.name: burgersConservativeUpdate
declarations.statement: def burgersConservativeUpdate (scale : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := conservativeUpdate scale u (burgersFlux u) i

declarations.name: totalOn_burgersConservativeUpdate
declarations.statement: theorem totalOn_burgersConservativeUpdate (scale : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : totalOn (fun i => burgersConservativeUpdate scale u i) n = totalOn u n - scale * (burgersFlux u n - burgersFlux u 0)

tasks.input: Translate this Burgers conservative finite-difference theorem into Lean 4: The finite total after a Burgers conservative update equals the old finite total minus scale times the boundary Burgers flux mismatch.
tasks.target: theorem totalOn_burgersConservativeUpdate (scale : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : totalOn (fun i => burgersConservativeUpdate scale u i) n = totalOn u n - scale * (burgersFlux u n - burgersFlux u 0)

tasks.input: Given the Burgers scaffold definitions and referenced conservation lemmas, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding burgersConservativeUpdate and applying totalOn_conservativeUpdate.

tasks.input: Find the generic total-balance theorem used for conservative finite-volume updates.
